Let me run the binary and examine it more thoroughly.

{"analysis":"The binary is a statically linked ELF binary (790KB). The strings output doesn't reveal much about the program's purpose - just compiler/library internals and CPU identification strings. I need to actually run the program and also try to disassemble/decompile it to understand what it does.","plan":"1. Run the program with no arguments to see output
2. Try with stdin input
3. Try with command line arguments
4. Install/check for disassembly tools like objdump or ghidra
5. Look for more specific strings like format specifiers","commands":[{"keystrokes":"./mystery
","duration":3},{"keystrokes":"echo hello | ./mystery
","duration":3},{"keystrokes":"./mystery hello
","duration":3},{"keystrokes":"strings -n 6 /app/mystery | grep -iE 'flag|key|secret|message|input|output|error|usage|help|arg|num|str|text|read|write|print|file|open|close|encrypt|decrypt|hash|encode|decode|compress|decompress|echo|reverse|sort|count|word|line|char|byte' | head -50
